commands.author: rohitjavvadi

target.title: Discord mention aliases rewrite @handle inside fenced code when body contains triple-backtick literal
target.body:
## Problem

Discord outbound mention rewriting can convert a plain `@handle` into a real `<@USER_ID>` mention while the handle is still inside a Markdown fenced code block.

The docs say `mentionAliases` leaves mentions inside Markdown code spans unchanged. The current fence detector is fooled when the fenced code body itself contains a triple-backtick literal, so the remainder of the real code block is treated as ordinary text and is passed through mention rewriting.

## Source evidence

- `extensions/discord/src/mentions.ts:11` uses `MARKDOWN_CODE_SEGMENT_PATTERN = /```[\s\S]*?```|`[^`\n]*`/g`.
- `extensions/discord/src/mentions.ts:142-148` rewrites text between regex matches with `rewritePlainTextMentions`.
- `extensions/discord/src/send.outbound.ts:179-183` calls `rewriteDiscordKnownMentions` for normal Discord sends.
- `extensions/discord/src/send.outbound.ts:443-449` calls it for structured send content.
- `extensions/discord/src/send.webhook.ts:89-92` calls it for webhook sends.
- `extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.process.ts:736-744` uses the rewritten text to decide whether a Discord final preview edit must become a fresh create because targeted mentions only ping on create.
- `docs/channels/discord.md:981` says unknown handles, `@everyone`, `@here`, and mentions inside Markdown code spans are left unchanged.

## Reproduction

From the repo root on current `main`:

```bash
node --import tsx --input-type=module - <<'NODE'
import assert from 'node:assert/strict';
import { rewriteDiscordKnownMentions } from './extensions/discord/src/mentions.ts';
import { resetDiscordDirectoryCacheForTest, rememberDiscordDirectoryUser } from './extensions/discord/src/directory-cache.ts';

resetDiscordDirectoryCacheForTest();
rememberDiscordDirectoryUser({
  accountId: 'default',
  userId: '123456789',
  handles: ['alice'],
});

const control = '```js\n// do not ping @alice\n```';
const bug = '```js\nconst fence = "```";\n// do not ping @alice\n```';
const controlActual = rewriteDiscordKnownMentions(control, { accountId: 'default' });
const bugActual = rewriteDiscordKnownMentions(bug, { accountId: 'default' });

assert.equal(controlActual, control, 'plain fenced code should keep @alice literal');
assert.notEqual(bugActual, bug, 'current bug repro: code-fenced @alice was rewritten');
assert.match(bugActual, /<@123456789>/, 'bug output contains a targeted Discord mention');

console.log(JSON.stringify({ controlActual, bugActual }, null, 2));
NODE
```

Actual output:

```json
{
  "controlActual": "```js\n// do not ping @alice\n```",
  "bugActual": "```js\nconst fence = \"```\";\n// do not ping <@123456789>\n```"
}
```

The control case preserves `@alice` inside a simple fenced block. The repro case rewrites `@alice` inside the same displayed fenced block because the scanner treats the `"` + ` ``` ` + `"` string literal as the closing fence.

## Expected behavior

Mention rewriting should not convert handles that are inside Markdown code spans or fenced code blocks. A triple-backtick sequence inside a fenced block body should not terminate the skipped code segment unless it is a real closing fence line.

## Impact

With a configured `mentionAliases` entry or a cached Discord directory handle, an assistant response that includes Markdown-about-Markdown, JavaScript snippets, shell examples, or other self-referential code can send an unintended targeted Discord ping inside code. The preview/final path can also make a fresh create decision based on the accidentally inserted targeted mention.

## Suggested fix direction

Replace the Discord mention code-region regex with a Markdown-aware scanner that pairs fenced block openings with closing fence runs on their own line, with closing length equal to or greater than the opener. Add regression coverage for:

- a triple-backtick literal inside a fenced block body followed by `@handle`;
- a fenced block opened with four backticks that contains a triple-backtick line;
- inline code spans with multi-backtick delimiters containing shorter backtick runs.

## Checks run

```bash
node --import tsx --input-type=module <repro above>
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/discord/src/mentions.test.ts --reporter dot
```

The focused test file passed: 1 file, 15 tests.

## Duplicate / overlap check

I searched issues and PRs for:

- `Discord mention code fence triple backticks`
- `rewriteDiscordKnownMentions code fence`
- `Discord @ mention inside code block`
- `MARKDOWN_CODE_SEGMENT_PATTERN`
- open PRs touching `extensions/discord/src/mentions.ts`

I did not find an issue or PR for this exact bug. The closest issue I found was closed #67587, which tracks the general mention-alias conversion feature rather than this code-fence over-rewrite. Open PR #90237 is adjacent mention-policy work but does not touch `extensions/discord/src/mentions.ts` or this fix shape.
